name: Penguin
status: Please step away from the penguin
verdict: Tux, Tencent QQ, Gentoo’s namesake, and Freedo (Linux-libre) keep this ice crowded.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Elephant
verdict: PostgreSQL, PHP, Hadoop, Evernote, Salesforce Ruth, Gradlephant, and CNCF Tai share this enclosure.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Owl
verdict: Duolingo, Hootsuite, TripAdvisor, Captain Kube, and Owlina keep the wise branch overcrowded.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Whale
verdict: Docker stacked containers on it. Separating the two now would require heavy machinery.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Fox
verdict: Firefox claimed the obvious one, Salesforce added Brandy, and GitLab keeps adding tanuki-related confusion.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Cat
verdict: Scratch, Octocat, Tomcat, Yarn's official kitten, and kitty terminal keep this enclosure loud.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Crab
verdict: Technically unofficial. Spiritually inseparable. Mention a crab near programmers and someone will explain memory safety.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Gopher
verdict: One project, near-total occupation. The little blue gopher has a very effective monopoly on developer affection.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Dolphin
verdict: MySQL named her Sakila. Pick this for a database and everyone will know what query you copied.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Monkey
verdict: Email, surveys and 3D modeling have formed an unlikely primate alliance.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Duck
verdict: One protects your searches. One used to handle your chats. Both got the duck before you.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Chameleon
verdict: SUSE has had this color-changing specialist in its enclosure for years.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Hedgehog
verdict: Hazel the Helm hedgehog rolled into this enclosure with chart in paw.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Dinosaur
verdict: Extinct in nature, surprisingly active in developer tooling.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Beaver
verdict: A database tool took the hardest-working animal. Honestly, the branding writes itself.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Cockroach
verdict: CockroachDB picked resilience over cuteness. It worked disturbingly well.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Red Panda
verdict: Redpanda the streaming company owns the name. Kotlin’s Kodee is a cute creature, not a red panda — so it does not count here.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Lion
verdict: Brave made the lion its browser face. Majestic, obvious and therefore rather crowded.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Otter
verdict: Otter.ai is here, but the enclosure is large and everyone appears to be holding hands.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Capybara
verdict: Calm, sociable and universally liked—the exact opposite of most engineering stand-ups. Go for it.

name: Pangolin
verdict: Armored, unusual and rolls away from bad meetings. A premium tech mascot waiting to happen.

name: Tapir
verdict: Looks like several animals were merged during a bad deploy. Perfect for tech.

name: Quokka
verdict: Ridiculously cheerful. Ideal until your first production incident.

name: Axolotl
verdict: The internet loves it, but big tech has not fully moved into the aquarium yet.

name: Camel
verdict: Perl chose the camel back when websites still had visitor counters.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Puffin
verdict: PrestaShop has a sharp-suited puffin named Preston. There is still room on the cliff.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Pufferfish
verdict: OpenBSD picked a fish that expands under threat. Security branding achieved.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Rabbit
verdict: Plan 9's Glenda shares the warren with RabbitMQ — still no invented runtime rabbits.

name: Squirrel
verdict: Streaming data, digital painting and Salesforce automation share one productive squirrel habitat.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Mule
verdict: A relic from the golden age of downloading one mysterious file for three days.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Pigeon
verdict: Pidgin gave instant messaging a purple pigeon. Sensible? Not really. Memorable? Yes.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Parrot
verdict: Colorful, observant and already running penetration tests.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Panda
verdict: PandaDoc claimed the obvious document pun. The bamboo remains unlicensed.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Raccoon
verdict: Salesforce sent Astro the raccoon to guide everyone through Trailhead.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Shark
verdict: DigitalOcean put Sammy the shark in its cloud. Marine biology has filed no complaint.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Dog
verdict: A very good dog is already watching the servers. Probably knows about the outage.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Seal
verdict: MariaDB left the dolphin family and brought a sea lion-looking resident to shore.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Snake
verdict: Python made two stylized snakes one of programming’s most recognizable marks.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Hummingbird
verdict: Fast, colorful and already helping developers ship interfaces everywhere.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Mastodon
verdict: The decentralized social network took the extinct elephant cousin and made it extremely online.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Zebra
verdict: Zee the etcd zebra interned at the Kubernetes zoo and never left.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Bee
verdict: Apache took the hive metaphor all the way to the logo. Efficient, if a little buzzy.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Pig
verdict: A language for analyzing Hadoop data chose a pig and refused to overthink it.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Octopus
verdict: GitHub fused it with a cat. A normal octopus may still argue it is legally a different vibe.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Koala
verdict: Sleepy, recognizable and still waiting for a major developer tool to wake it up.

name: Lemur
verdict: Looks permanently surprised by the sprint backlog. Strong mascot energy.

name: Armadillo
verdict: Ships with its own physical firewall. Cybersecurity companies, take note.

name: Flamingo
verdict: Bright, balanced and capable of standing through meetings on one leg.

name: Moose
verdict: Huge antlers, calm presence, excellent silhouette. Somehow still underused.

name: Sloth
verdict: An honest mascot for any product whose performance work is “on the roadmap.”

name: Bat
verdict: sharkdp/bat claims the night shift: a cat clone with wings, and the name to match.

name: Badger
verdict: Persistent, striped and not easily discouraged by a failed funding round.

name: Alpaca
verdict: Cute and technically available, but everyone will hear “LLaMA” and start discussing models.

name: Kiwi
verdict: Compact, unusual and confidently flightless. A respectable Linux alternative.

name: Orca
verdict: Bold monochrome branding included by nature. Docker chose the gentler whale.

name: Narwhal
verdict: A whale with a built-in unicorn feature. Product differentiation solved.

name: Mantis
verdict: MantisBT has been patiently waiting in code review since the early web.

name: Beetle
status: Plenty of room under the log
verdict: Compact, armored and ships in many colors. Volkswagen aside, tech is oddly quiet.

name: Frog
verdict: JFrog hopped into this enclosure with an animal brand name and a large software supply-chain footprint.

name: Turtle
verdict: Cappy the Cluster API turtle moved into this previously empty pond.

name: Wombat
verdict: We could not verify a current official npm wombat mascot with a primary source, so this enclosure is open.

name: GNU / Wildebeest
verdict: GNU put the wildebeest at the center of free software long before mascots became a brand workshop exercise.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Weasel
verdict: The V language has Veasel, an officially certified cute weasel. The name practically deployed itself.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Antelope
verdict: Libreboot uses a leaping antelope silhouette and an excellent “Canteloupe” pun.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Butterfly
verdict: Raku’s Camelia is a colorful butterfly-like bug designed to celebrate diversity.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Firefly
verdict: Apache Beam gave distributed data processing a tiny glowing representative.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Ladybug
status: A couple of bugs filed claims
verdict: Two software projects independently discovered that a friendly bug is excellent bug-related branding.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Fish
verdict: FreeDOS has Blinky, proving even a DOS-compatible operating system can keep a pet fish.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Platypus
verdict: Apple’s Darwin project chose an animal assembled like an experimental operating system.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Pony
verdict: Pony chose a pony. Sometimes the naming meeting ends exactly when it should.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Dragon
verdict: KDE has Konqi, a green dragon who has survived more desktop redesigns than most humans.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Woodpecker
verdict: The Kate editor has a cyber woodpecker. Persistent tapping suddenly feels on-brand.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Cow
verdict: Gentoo’s community cow and TerminusDB’s cow-duck hybrid make this pasture stranger than expected.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Mouse
verdict: A desktop environment using a mouse: obvious, charming and almost suspiciously appropriate.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Bear
verdict: Salesforce made Codey the bear a champion for builders. The hard hat is implied.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Goat
status: Cloudy with a chance of SaaS
verdict: Cloudy the goat keeps Salesforce learners on track and presumably eats abandoned roadmaps.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Horse
verdict: We dropped an unsourced Salesforce Blaze claim. This pasture is open until a primary source lands.

name: Tanuki
verdict: Astro is a tanuki, and GitLab’s logo began with one. Everyone will still call both of them something else.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Thunderbird
verdict: Mozilla gave email a mythic bird wrapped around an envelope. Subtlety was not invited.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Cuttlefish
verdict: PeerTube has Sepia, a hat-wearing cuttlefish in the fediverse aquarium.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Iguana
verdict: The Zig community has not one but two iguana mascots. Apparently one was insufficiently ziggy.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Llama
verdict: It used to be a lovely available mascot. Then generative AI arrived with several billion parameters.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Giraffe
verdict: Phippy the giraffe teaches Kubernetes concepts while seeing over the entire cluster.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Aardvark
verdict: Excellent snout, strong alphabetical placement, no dominant tech resident spotted.

name: Bison
verdict: GNU Bison has been chewing through grammars since before npm existed.

name: Donkey
verdict: Peer-to-peer history left a mark, but the modern tech stable is fairly quiet.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Ferret
verdict: FerretDB took the name and the open-source MongoDB-compatible niche.

name: Gazelle
verdict: Bazel Gazelle generates BUILD files and owns this antelope-adjacent lane.

name: Gorilla
verdict: Gorilla Logic is in the enclosure, but no single tech gorilla rules the jungle.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Hippo
verdict: Looks friendly, runs surprisingly fast and would make an excellent infrastructure mascot.

name: Hyena
verdict: Distributed teamwork, excellent uptime and a laugh for every failed competitor deploy.

name: Ibex
verdict: lowRISC Ibex is a small RISC-V core with a sure-footed animal name.

name: Jackal
verdict: Clearpath Robotics named a rugged unmanned ground vehicle Jackal.

name: Kangaroo
verdict: Ships with built-in storage and excellent bounce rates. Marketing can take it from here.

name: Lynx
verdict: Lynx still prowls terminals as the classic text web browser.

name: Meerkat
verdict: Already doing observability: one watches while the others deploy.

name: Mole
verdict: Perfect for infrastructure that works quietly where nobody can see it.

name: Orangutan
verdict: Thoughtful, distinctive and much less crowded than the generic monkey branch.

name: Ox
verdict: Not glamorous, but it will keep processing long after the pitch deck crashes.

name: Rhino
verdict: Rhino 3D has a substantial claim in design software, but the wider tech savanna remains open.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Tiger
verdict: Apple used it for an operating-system era, but modern tech has largely left the jungle.
scoreOverrideRationale: Preserved from prototype editorial score during migration.

name: Walrus
verdict: Walrus parked decentralized storage under a distinguished animal name.

name: Wolf
verdict: Arctic Wolf runs security operations under a clear animal brand name.

name: Yak
verdict: Handles harsh environments and carries heavy loads. Infrastructure mascot perfection.

name: Eagle
verdict: Eagle Eye Networks took the aerial brand for cloud video security.

name: Raven
verdict: RavenDB nested here with an animal-named document database.

name: Lobster
verdict: Linky the Linkerd lobster brought AUTH'N and AUTH'Z tattoos to the zoo.

name: Macaw
verdict: Indigo the in-toto macaw guards the nuts — and the provenance tree.

name: Warthog
verdict: Grunt claimed the warthog logomark for JavaScript build chores.

name: Squid
verdict: Squid Cache has been proxying the web under cephalopod branding for decades.

name: Pelican
verdict: Pelican the static site generator filled its pouch with Python pages.

name: Puma
verdict: The Puma web server sprinted away with this big-cat name.

name: Condor
verdict: HTCondor soars over high-throughput computing clusters.

name: Kudu
verdict: Apache Kudu took the antelope for fast columnar storage.

name: Impala
verdict: Apache Impala runs analytic SQL at antelope speed.

name: Canary
verdict: Thinkst Canary turned the coal-mine bird into a tripwire for attackers.

name: Crane
verdict: Google's crane CLI moves container images with long-legged precision.

name: Ocelot
verdict: Ocelot the .NET API gateway prowls the reverse-proxy trail.

name: Chinchilla
verdict: DeepMind named a scaling-law paper after this soft-furred rodent.

name: Vicuna
verdict: LMSYS Vicuna is the chatty camelid next to LLaMA in the AI barn.

name: Falcon
verdict: TII Falcon LLM stooped on the open-weight bird niche.

name: Ant
verdict: Apache Ant has been hauling Java builds since before Maven was cool.

name: Firebird
verdict: Firebird SQL kept the mythical bird as a very real relational database.

name: Bowerbird
verdict: Bower named itself after birds that collect shiny front-end packages.

name: Anaconda
verdict: Fedora and RHEL install through Anaconda — a snake that wraps the whole disk.

name: Daemon
verdict: Beastie the BSD daemon is not a zoo animal, but FreeBSD keeps him on the grounds anyway.
